**Emoji familiarity (infomation screen)**

I use and receive them daily; I use and receive them a few times a week; I use and receive them sometimes; I rarely or never use them, but I receive them daily; I rarely or never use them, but I receive them a few times a week; I rarely or never use them, but I receive them sometimes; I never receive nor use them.

**(explain1.html)**

Welcome!

In this experiment, you will see sentences followed by emojis and you must provide judgments regarding the text-image relationship.

Follow the instructions carefully. During this stage, you may freely go back and forth through the slides.

**(explain2.html)**

For each sentence-emoji combination, you will have to answer, on a scale of 1 to 7, to what extent:

a) the emoji is visually identifiable;
b) the emoji and the sentence make sense together;
c) the emoji adds information to the sentence.

**(explain3_1.html)**

a\) To what extent is the emoji visually identifiable?

By "visually identifiable", we refer to the fact that some emojis have a clearer design than others. For example:

![](apple.png){fig-align="center"}

The emoji above depicts an apple in a reasonably typical way. Most people, therefore, would quickly recognize an apple and rate it 7 (perfectly identifiable) or close to 7 in this regard.

**(explain3_2.html)**

a\) To what extent is the emoji visually identifiable?

![](worm.png){fig-align="center"}

Here, on the other hand, some people might indeed see a worm and give high scores (7 or close to it), but a good portion would probably be unsure about what is being depicted (a worm? a snake? a tail?) and would rate it a 1 (impossible to identify) or close to it.

**(explain4.html)**

b\) To what extent do the emoji and the sentence make sense together?

By the sentence and emoji "making sense together", we mean that some sentence-emoji pairs will make more sense than others for each participant.

If you think that the emoji and the sentence, interpreted together, make no sense at all, you should rate it a 1 or close to a 1. On the other hand, if you think the sentence and the emoji make perfect sense together, you should rate it a 7 or close to a 7.

**(explain5_1.html)**

c\) To what extent does the emoji add information to the sentence?

Finally, by "adding information," we mean that the emoji can be redundant regarding the message or add some contextual element to it.

![I took my car to the mechanic\*](mechanic.png){fig-align="center"}

Above, for example, there seems to be a certain repetition. The emoji at the end, portraying a tool, appears only to refer back to the context established by "mechanic". If you think the same, here you would rate it a 1 (adds no information) or close to a 1.

**(explain5_2.html)**

c\) To what extent does the emoji add information to the sentence?

![I cheered a lot for him today\*](race.png){fig-align="center"}

A different case occurs here. Many would see a connection between the car at the end and the event where the "cheering" takes place. Without the emoji, the sentence would not have this information. It would be a situation where the emoji adds a lot of information or even essential information for understanding the sentence. If you see this connection, here you would rate it a 7 or close to a 7.

**(explain6.html)**

The important thing, in all cases, is not to overthink. Answer according to what comes to mind first, without trying to construct complex scenarios. 

The explanations provided mostly indicate the extremes of each category. 

At times you might prefer to use the intermediate numbers. 

There is no right or wrong answer. We are interested in your individual judgment.

**(explain_final.html)**

Now that you've read the instructions, shall we do a practice round?

**(training1)**
I've left the door open 🚪

**(training_unused)** - Ultimately this one was not added.
I cheered a lot for him today 🪱

**(training3)**
I didn't finish the project 🧶

**(start.html)**
Ok! Now that you've completed the training, let's begin the experiment.

Remember to proceed without overthinking; however, do the task carefully - your answers are very important to us!

Please, when you begin, remain focused until the end of the task. The experiment lasts around 15 minutes. There will be a break in the middle. The data is only saved at the end.

**

**(break)**
You've completed the first half of the experiment. Take a break if you want and click Continue to proceed.

**(final)**
Thank you for participating! Your answers were recorded.
